summary: Previewed app-builder ontology SourcePlan with 30 file(s) and 0 issue(s).

value.displayText: App-builder SourcePlan preview: root=., template=none, target=none, source=application-assembly, files=30, witnesses=243, controlUses=15, expectedEffects=18, handoffNotes=7, decisionBundles=5, issues=0.

handoffNotes.summary: Concrete source is a recommendable starting scaffold unless an existing app already owns that structure.
Generated seed records came from explicit app-builder input and keep the app runnable; replace them or connect them to real persistence when moving beyond scaffold data.
Generated service source is a local service/adaptor scaffold for Aurelia wiring and semantic verification; server APIs, fetch policy, caching, retries, and persistence remain caller/AI-owned.

App-builder generated Aurelia source structure, bindings, DI/router wiring, and selected source-lowering mechanics; ordinary TypeScript domain rules and business policy remain caller/AI-owned unless they are explicit generated source facts.

To materialize this preview, write the 30/30 returned sourcePlan.files row(s) that include complete text under ., apply returned projectTooling/package setup when present, then reopen the app through semantic-runtime; this query does not write files.

ApplicationAssembly composes runnable app topology and route/service areas from explicit child source plans; generated DI state collections share an assembly-level state boundary, while service persistence, external data, and caller-owned business invariants remain outside the declared expected semantic effects.

This SourcePlan declares 18 expected semantic effect(s), 15 generated control-use row(s), and generated source/tooling witnesses for verification after materialization; the preview itself has not written files or reopened the generated app.

pattern.title: Application assembly
pattern.summary: One Aurelia entrypoint and root route shell coordinating several generated routed app areas.
adaptationNotes: The root app shell owns startup, router admission, top-level navigation, and shared viewport placement.
Child route areas own their domain state, route components, nested detail viewports, and service boundaries.
This pattern is the first app-builder rung where multiple app areas must compose without duplicating root tooling.

modules.title: Aurelia app shell
modules.summary: Public Aurelia entrypoint plus root component/template ownership.

modules.title: Convention resource admission
modules.summary: Class/file/template naming is intentionally compatible with Aurelia convention resource discovery.

modules.title: Router shell
modules.summary: RouterConfiguration, route config, navigation links, and au-viewport shell wiring.

modules.title: Route link navigation
modules.summary: Templates create static or data-driven route instructions instead of string-only DOM href plumbing.

modules.title: Nested viewport layout
modules.summary: A child route area owns its own au-viewport so nested route components render inside the active parent route component.

modules.title: DI state boundary
modules.summary: Components resolve ordinary state/domain classes through Aurelia DI instead of passing facades around.

modules.title: Ordinary domain classes
modules.summary: Domain entities and value objects are modeled as ordinary classes with readable properties and getters.

modules.title: List rendering
modules.summary: Repeated rows/cards/lists intentionally exercise item-scope template-controller handoff.

modules.title: Template-controller flow
modules.summary: If, repeat, switch, promise, or let scopes are intentional semantic structure rather than markup decoration.
